text:
Program:AM

Presented by

Congratulations to Australia's Olympic athletes in Paris - they've achieved the country's biggest ever gold medal haul - winning 18 medals.

The Prime Minister has promised them a hero's welcome when they return.

Paris 2024 Olympics - Athletics - Women's Pole Vault Final - Stade de France, Saint-Denis, France - August 07, 2024. Nina Kennedy of Australia celebrates with her national flag after winning gold REUTERS/Kai Pfaffenbach

Sabra Lane:Congratulations to Australia's Olympic athletes in Paris. They've achieved the country's biggest gold medal haul in our history, winning 18 medals with several days of competition still to go. The prime minister's promising a hero's welcome with the Olympians return next week. Kathleen Ferguson reports.

Kathleen Ferguson :There's a golden glow across the country this morning. Australians woke up to the news that its Olympic athletes have added four medals to their gold medal haul in Paris. Keegan Palmer broke the tally record with his win in the men's park skating. Keegan Palmer is now a double Olympic gold medalist.

Keegan Palmer:I was a two-time Olympian and now we're a two-time gold medalist. It doesn't really get much better than me. Especially on the day Australia's had today, we broke our record. And to be able to be a part of that is just incredible.

Kathleen Ferguson :It was a big day for our athletes. Nina Kennedy won gold in the women's pole vault. Matt Wern also won back-to-back gold by taking out the men's dinghy in the sailing. In the cycling, Australia also triumphed in the men's team pursuit for the first time since the 2004 Athens Olympics. The Prime Minister, Anthony Albanese, says they've all made the country proud and they can expect a big welcome home next week.

Anthony Albanese:They deserve absolutely every accolade that we can give them because it is uplifting at a time where there's so much conflict and turmoil and bad news in the world. What they've given Australians is a great deal of pleasure and pride.

Kathleen Ferguson :With several days of competition left, Australia is third on the gold medal table, trailing the United States and China. We must point out we're beating Great Britain. Our athletes are still in contention in a number of other sports, including our track cyclists, the Opals in the women's basketball and the women's beach volleyball team. The only complication in what has been a great event for Australia, hockey player Tom Craig has apologised after being released without charge for attempting to buy cocaine.

Sabra Lane:Kathleen Ferguson there.

title: Australia hauls in record number of Olympic Gold Medals - ABC listen
description: Congratulations to Australia's Olympic athletes in Paris - they've achieved the country's biggest ever gold medal haul - winning 18 medals.
keywords: Podcast, olympics, paris 2024, australia, keegan palmer, nina kennedy
